mappings.rationale: Zalasiewicz covers the physical features of the Earth's surface including continental structure, oceanic ridges, mountain ranges, and the tectonic processes that shape them.
Molnar discusses the tectonic processes creating oceanic ridges, deep-sea trenches, mountain ranges, and volcanic island arcs—all major features described in this section.

mappings.vsiAuthor: John A. Matthews, David T. Herbert
mappings.rationale: Matthews and Herbert cover the physiography of continents and oceans, the distribution of land and sea, and the physical geography of the Earth's surface.
Redfern provides an overview of the Earth's surface features including continental and oceanic regions, mountain systems, and their geological origins.
Goudie and Viles cover the physical landscape features of the Earth's surface including mountains, plateaus, rift valleys, and coastal landforms discussed in this section.
Stow covers oceanic physiography including continental margins, oceanic ridges, deep-sea trenches, abyssal plains, and seamounts—core topics of this section.
Price covers mountain types, the worldwide system of mountain ranges, and mountain formation processes directly described in this section.
Lowrie discusses the geophysical properties of continental shields, oceanic crust, and the processes underlying the formation of mountains and ocean basins.
Sheppard covers coral islands, coral reefs, and atolls, directly corresponding to the section's treatment of these features.
Directly relevant to volcanic mountains, volcanic island arcs, and the Circum-Pacific System of volcanic activity described in this section.
Lenton provides context for understanding the Earth's surface features as products of interacting Earth system processes.
Dodds covers Antarctic physiography and the continental features of Antarctica, relevant to the section's treatment of continental physiography.
Covers Arctic continental shelf features, oceanic ridges, and the physiography of the Arctic region relevant to this section.
Dodds discusses the geopolitical significance of physical features including continental shelves, ocean basins, and maritime boundaries shaped by Earth's surface features.
